reasoning: The rule requires TLS-compliant applications to implement all seven mandatory extensions: supported_versions, cookie, signature_algorithms, signature_algorithms_cert, supported_groups, key_share, and server_name. The two primary functions implement the cookie extension on the server side: tls_parse_ctos_cookie (lines 1008-1219) handles parsing the cookie extension from ClientHello including HMAC verification, format validation, and transcript reconstruction, while tls_construct_stoc_cookie (lines 2085-2215) handles constructing the cookie extension in HelloRetryRequest. The other six required extensions are also implemented in the codebase: tls_parse_ctos_server_name at ssl/statem/extensions_srvr.c:113, tls_parse_ctos_sig_algs at ssl/statem/extensions_srvr.c:311, tls_parse_ctos_sig_algs_cert at ssl/statem/extensions_srvr.c:284, tls_parse_ctos_supported_groups at ssl/statem/extensions_srvr.c:1221, tls_parse_ctos_key_share at ssl/statem/extensions_srvr.c:830, and tls_construct_ctos_supported_versions at ssl/statem/extensions_clnt.c:733. All seven mandatory extensions have both client-side construction and server-side parsing implementations registered in the extension table at ssl/statem/extensions.c.

evidence.expected_behavior: All seven mandatory TLS 1.3 extensions (supported_versions, cookie, signature_algorithms, signature_algorithms_cert, supported_groups, key_share, server_name) should be implemented
